news.title:
Experts recommend 'Buy' for Gujarat Gas citing strong fundamentals but short-term margin pressures; 'Hold' for BPCL due to crude oil price volatility.
ICICI Prudential, UltraTech Cement, Tata Capital, and Paradeep Phosphates are advised as 'Hold' with sector-specific cautions and stop-loss strategies.
news.summary:
Experts recommend 'Buy' for Gujarat Gas citing strong fundamentals but short-term margin pressures; 'Hold' for BPCL due to crude oil price volatility.

news.title:
Gujarat Gas Ltd hits a 52-week low of Rs 302.7 after 5 consecutive losses.
The stock is down 40.5% from its 52-week high of Rs 508.6 amid market weakness.
news.summary:

news.title:
India's PNG expansion and green hydrogen blending create long-term demand for gas and hydrogen players.
GAIL and Gujarat Gas to benefit from hydrogen blending; NTPC gains as an early mover in the segment.

news.title:
Morbi's 700 ceramic units shut for 30 days due to gas shortages, export disruptions.
Gujarat Gas Co Ltd affected as units plan price hikes post-shutdown amid Iran conflict.

news.title:
Gujarat Gas requested industrial clients to assess April gas usage for supply management.
Gas prices surged to Rs 85-88+VAT from Rs 41.3+VAT; production may halt if allocation is below 70%.
news.summary:

news.title:
CLSA notes Middle East war impacts stocks like ONGC, Oil India, Reliance, and OMCs differently.
Post-war, upstream firms like ONGC may gain, while OMCs face margin pressure if crude stays elevated.
news.summary:

news.title:
Oil marketing stocks HPCL, BPCL, and Indian Oil plunged 25-30% due to the US-Iran war, with valuations below 5-year averages.
Gas stocks like Petronet LNG, GAIL, IGL, MGL, and Gujarat Gas dropped 11-27%, while ONGC and Oil India fell 3-4% but stayed above 5-year averages.
news.summary:
Oil marketing stocks HPCL, BPCL, and Indian Oil plunged 25-30% due to the US-Iran war, with valuations below 5-year averages.
Gas stocks like Petronet LNG, GAIL, IGL, MGL, and Gujarat Gas dropped 11-27%, while ONGC and Oil India fell 3-4% but stayed above 5-year averages.

A 20% curtailment may cut Gujarat Gas volumes by 10%, MGL, IGL by 3%.

Debt-free status, superior liquidity, and internal accruals support capex and rating stability.
